title: mentha genevieri
intro: Mentha genevieri, also known as Corn Mint or Pennyroyal, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve digestive issues and alleviate menstrual cramps. The essential oil extracted from the plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including headaches, nausea, and respiratory issues, and has also been known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Mentha genevieri is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topicals, and can be consumed in capsule or tincture form to reap its medicinal benefits.
